communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Subject: Public Records Request: Emergency Medical Records & CAD Logs - Incident #110004917

To the Boston EMS Public Records Officer,

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law (M.G.L. c. 66, § 10), I hereby request access to all records maintained by Boston EMS regarding the emergency response to 141 W. Newton St, Boston, MA, on January 4, 2011, associated with Boston Police Incident Report #110004917.

Specifically, I am requesting:

1. All Computer-Aided Dispatch (CAD) logs or equivalent dispatch records generated by Boston EMS for this incident.

2. Any and all Emergency Medical Service (EMS) run reports, patient care reports (PCRs), or incident summary sheets generated by the responding EMS personnel at this location and time.

3. Any audio logs of dispatch communications regarding this incident.

Context: I have already filed a request with the Boston Police Department (BPD) regarding this incident, and they indicated they have no responsive records and directed me to Boston EMS. As Boston EMS is the primary agency for medical dispatch and response, these records should be within your custody and control.

If any portion of these records is deemed exempt under HIPAA or other statutes, please redact only the specific protected health information (PHI) and provide the remainder of the records as required by the Massachusetts Public Records Law.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Iran Archive
